meta.title: C253 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Mercedes GLC-Class C253 engine — M274, M264, M276, M177, OM651, OM654, OM642, OM656. Real UK costs, honest reliability data, and GLC 63 V8 explained. Get free quotes from 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes GLC-Class C253 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned C253 engine typically costs £1,800–£18,000 supply only, depending on engine — the volume M274 four-cylinder petrol sits at £1,800–£4,200, while the M177 V8 in the AMG GLC 63 S is the most expensive at £12,000–£18,000. Fitting adds £650–£1,500 depending on engine complexity and specialist rates. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes GLC-Class C253 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M274 2.0-litre four-cylinder petrol (GLC 300, 245 PS) is widely considered the most reliable engine in the C253 range — strong performance, proven durability, and no major systemic failures when properly maintained. The OM654 2.0-litre diesel (GLC 220 d, 194 PS) introduced at facelift is also highly regarded — quieter, cleaner, and more economical than the OM651 it replaced.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes GLC-Class C253?

acceptedAnswer.text: Camshaft adjuster wear on the M274/M264 petrol fours, cooling system leaks on the M276 V6 (GLC 43), injector seal failure ("Black Death") on the OM651 diesel (pre-facelift), and valve stem seal wear on the M177 V8 (GLC 63). The M276 V6 is particularly sensitive to cooling system neglect, and the OM651 requires injector seal attention around 80,000–130,000 miles.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes GLC-Class C253 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Overall reliability is good for a modern premium SUV coupé. The M274, M264, and OM654 are proven, durable units. The main caveats are the M276's cooling system, the OM651's injector seals (pre-facelift), and the M177's valve stem seals — all are known issues that should be budgeted for. Facelift cars (2019+) with M264 and OM654 engines are newer and generally more refined.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes GLC-Class C253 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine outright — but the M276 V6 (GLC 43) has documented cooling system issues that can be expensive to address. The M177 V8 (GLC 63) has valve stem seal wear and is eye-wateringly expensive to repair. If you're on a budget, the four-cylinder GLC 300 is the sweet spot. Avoid any pre-facelift OM651 diesel without documented injector service history — "Black Death" repairs can be costly.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my C253 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cold-start rattle on petrol engines (camshaft adjusters), persistent coolant loss on the V6 (cooling system leaks), black carbon buildup around injectors on pre-facelift diesels ("Black Death"), or blue smoke on startup on the GLC 63 (valve stem seals) are the key warning signs. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes GLC-Class C253 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value. Against a well-kept £25,000 GLC 300, a £2,200–£4,000 recon is 9–16% of the car's value — viable. Against a tired £12,000 GLC 220 d, a £2,000–£3,800 recon is 17–32% — still viable. For a GLC 63 S, a £40,000+ car justifies a £12,000–£18,000 recon; a £25,000 example makes it a much harder call. Our 70% Mercedes-Benz threshold is the line — most C253s sit comfortably above it.

mainEntity.name: How long do Mercedes GLC-Class C253 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M274 four-cylinder petrol can exceed 150,000–200,000 miles with regular servicing and prompt camshaft adjuster attention. The OM654 diesel is similarly durable — 200,000+ miles is achievable with proper AdBlue system care. The M177 V8 can last 150,000+ miles but typically requires valve stem seal replacement at 30,000–70,000 miles, which is a significant cost.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the C253 and X253 GLC-Class?

acceptedAnswer.text: Both are the first-generation GLC-Class (2015–2022). The X253 is the conventional SUV body style (2015–2022). The C253 is the coupé SUV body style (2016–2022, this page), with a raked roofline and sleeker styling. Engines are shared across both body styles, though the coupé adds additional weight and slightly reduced cargo capacity. See the X253 page for the SUV's specifics.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes GLC-Class C253 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate (under the bonnet or on the driver's door pillar), on the engine block, and in the vehicle documentation. Tell us your variant (GLC 200, GLC 250, GLC 300, GLC 43, GLC 63, GLC 63 S, GLC 220 d, GLC 250 d, GLC 300 d, GLC 350 d, GLC 400 d) and approximate model year (pre-2019 or post-2019 facelift) and we'll identify it — 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists will confirm it for free.

hero.h1: Mercedes GLC-Class C253 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the first-generation GLC Coupé — from the M274 four-cylinder petrol to the M276 V6, the M177 V8 that powers the GLC 63, and the OM654 diesel that replaced the OM651 at facelift. Real UK costs, honest reliability data, and the 2019 facelift's engine changes explained. Backed by real UK enquiries in 2025 [PATTERN DATA].

engineDatabase.h2: Mercedes GLC-Class C253 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the C253 coupé SUV specifically — including pre-facelift M274/M276/OM651 units and post-facelift M264/M256/OM654 replacements.

overview.intro: The C253 is the coupé variant of the first-generation Mercedes-Benz GLC-Class, produced from 2016 to 2022 as the sleeker, more sporting counterpart to the X253 SUV. Built on the MRA platform shared with the C-Class, it combines GLC practicality with a raked roofline. The engine range spanned M274 four-cylinder petrols, M276 V6 petrols in AMG GLC 43 guise, the M177 V8 in GLC 63 variants, plus OM651, OM654, OM642 and OM656 diesel units. A 2019 facelift brought M264 engines with EQ Boost mild-hybrid technology, the OM654 diesel, and the range-topping GLC 400 d.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: Mercedes MRA (Modular Rear Architecture) • Predecessor: None (first GLC Coupé) • Successor: Mercedes-Benz GLC Coupé (X254/C254) • Sibling body styles in this generation: X253 SUV (2015–2022).
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The C253 GLC Coupé generated UK engine enquiries in 2025 [PATTERN DATA], with the M274 petrol fours and OM651 diesels remaining the most commonly discussed among specialists.

items.quote: Thin-Data Fallback mode — one paragraph: The C253's most dependable engine is the M274 2.0-litre four-cylinder petrol (GLC 300, 245 PS) — strong performance, proven durability, and no major systemic failures when properly maintained. The OM654 2.0-litre diesel (GLC 220 d, 194 PS) introduced at facelift is also highly regarded — quieter, cleaner, and more economical than the OM651 it replaced. The M276 V6 in the GLC 43 offers effortless performance but carries higher running costs and is sensitive to cooling system neglect. The M177 V8 in the GLC 63 S (510 PS) is an icon — epic performance but eye-watering maintenance bills. Avoid any pre-facelift OM651 diesel without documented injector service history, and be cautious of the M276 V6's cooling system — a known weakness on the GLC 43.

rows.commonMajorFailure: Valve stem seals, coolant pipes, engine mounts
rows.ownershipVerdict: V8 Icon — epic engine, epic bills

Proven V6 Diesel — older but robust

economicsRule.title: The C253 rule
economicsRule.text: A £2,200–£4,200 recon on a sub-£15,000 C253 sits at 15–28% of the car's value — viable on most examples. Against a £6,000 engine bill on a GLC 43, the car's value determines the decision — a £30,000 GLC 43 justifies a £4,000–£7,000 recon; a £15,000 example makes it a much harder call. For a GLC 63 S, a £40,000+ car justifies a £12,000–£18,000 recon. Our 70% Mercedes-Benz threshold is the line — most C253s sit comfortably above it.

keyTakeaways.question: Which C253 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: GLC 220 d (OM651) — proven diesel, excellent economy, cheap parts.

keyTakeaways.question: Which C253 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: AMG GLC 63 S (M177) — V8 complexity, specialist-only work, eye-watering parts prices.

keyTakeaways.question: Which C253 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: GLC 300 (M274.920) — 245 PS, reliable four-pot, strong performance without V6 running costs.

problems.title: M274/M264 Petrol Camshaft Adjuster Wear
problems.affectedModels: C253 GLC 200, GLC 250, GLC 300 (M274 and M264 petrol variants).
problems.rootCause: The M274 and M264's camshaft adjusters can wear prematurely, causing a rattle on cold start and, in severe cases, timing chain stretch. This is particularly common on cars with extended oil change intervals.

tieredCosts.work: Replace camshaft adjusters and timing chain tensioner
Full timing chain and adjuster replacement

problems.recommendation: If your C253 petrol has a rattle on cold start, have the camshaft adjusters inspected immediately — £700–£1,200 is far cheaper than the £2,200+ bill that follows if the timing chain stretches or jumps. Use the correct oil spec and avoid extended service intervals.

cta.label: Compare M274/M264 engine prices for your C253 GLC-Class →

problems.title: M276 V6 Cooling System Leaks
problems.affectedModels: C253 AMG GLC 43 (all M276 V6 variants).
problems.rootCause: The M276 V6's cooling system — particularly the water pump, thermostat housing, and coolant crossover pipes — is prone to leaks. The V6 layout makes access tight, increasing labour costs. The facelift's power increase to 390 PS doesn't change the underlying cooling system architecture.

tieredCosts.work: Replace water pump, thermostat, and coolant pipes
Full cooling system overhaul + engine inspection if overheated

problems.recommendation: Check coolant levels regularly and look for signs of leaks around the water pump and thermostat housing — £500–£900 in preventative work is cheap insurance against the £2,000+ bill that follows an overheat. This is the single most important job on any M276-powered C253.

cta.label: Compare M276 engine prices for your C253 GLC 43 →

problems.title: OM651 Diesel Injector Seal Failure ("Black Death")
problems.affectedModels: C253 GLC 220 d, GLC 250 d (pre-facelift OM651 diesel variants).
problems.rootCause: The OM651's injector seals can fail, allowing combustion gases to escape past the seal — this creates a black, carbon-like buildup around the injector (hence "Black Death"). Left untreated, it can damage the cylinder head and require expensive repairs.

tieredCosts.work: Replace injector seals and clean carbon deposits
Cylinder head repair or replacement if damaged

problems.recommendation: Have the injector seals inspected at every service after 80,000 miles — £400–£700 in preventative work is a fraction of the £1,800+ bill that follows if the cylinder head is damaged. Use premium diesel and quality injector cleaner.

cta.label: Compare OM651 engine prices for your C253 diesel →

problems.title: M177 V8 Valve Stem Seal Wear (GLC 63 / GLC 63 S)
problems.affectedModels: C253 AMG GLC 63, AMG GLC 63 S (all M177 V8 variants).
problems.rootCause: The M177's hot-vee design — turbos located in the V of the engine — generates significant heat, which can cause valve stem seals to harden and fail. This leads to oil consumption, blue smoke on startup, and eventually, more serious engine damage if ignored.

tieredCosts.work: Full engine rebuild if oil starvation has caused bottom-end damage

problems.recommendation: If your GLC 63 is smoking on startup or using oil between services, have the valve stem seals inspected immediately — a £2,000–£3,500 repair is far cheaper than the £10,000+ rebuild that follows. Budget for this as a preventative job at 50,000 miles if you plan to keep the car long-term.

cta.label: Compare M177 engine prices for your C253 GLC 63 →

replacementCosts.h2: C253 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are C253 coupé SUV only. X253 SUV shares most of these engines but is covered on its own Generation Page, since body style affects weight, aerodynamics, and to some extent, labour access. The C253 was produced from June 2016 to 2022, with a facelift in 2019.
